inner-join.label: INNER JOIN
inner-join.summary: Retrieve matching rows from two tables.

sketch-what-is-inner-join.title: What is an INNER JOIN?
sketch-what-is-inner-join.bodyMarkdown: An **INNER JOIN** in SQL combines rows from two tables based on a related column between them. Only rows with matching values in both tables are included in the result. For example, if you want to see which students are enrolled in which courses, you can join the `students` and `enrollments` tables using the `student_id` column.

sketch-inner-join-example.title: INNER JOIN Example
sketch-inner-join-example.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you want to list all students and the courses they are enrolled in. You can use an INNER JOIN between `students` and `enrollments`:

```sql
SELECT students.name, enrollments.term
FROM students
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
```

This query returns only students who have enrollments, showing their names and the term for each enrollment.

sketch-join-multiple-tables.title: Joining Multiple Tables
sketch-join-multiple-tables.bodyMarkdown:
You can join more than two tables using INNER JOIN. For example, to get the student name, course title, and department name for each enrollment, you can join `students`, `enrollments`, `courses`, and `departments`:

```sql
SELECT students.name, courses.title, departments.name
FROM enrollments
INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id
INNER JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id
INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
```

This query shows how INNER JOIN can connect related data across several tables.

quiz-inner-join-basic.title: INNER JOIN Result
quiz-inner-join-basic.prompt: What does an INNER JOIN return?
quiz-inner-join-basic.hint: Think about what happens when there is no match.

help.concept: INNER JOIN returns only rows where the join condition is true for both tables.
help.hint_1: Rows are included only if there is a match in both tables.
help.hint_2: If a row in one table does not have a matching row in the other, it will not appear in the result.

options.a: All rows from the left table, matched or not
options.b: All rows from the right table, matched or not
options.c: Only rows with matching values in both tables
options.d: All possible combinations of rows from both tables

quiz-inner-join-syntax.title: INNER JOIN Syntax Order
quiz-inner-join-syntax.prompt: Arrange the SQL keywords to form a correct INNER JOIN statement.
quiz-inner-join-syntax.hint: Start with the correct answer, then specify the tables and join condition.

help.concept: The basic structure of an the correct answer query is the correct answer, the correct answer, the correct answer, the correct answer.
help.hint_1: You the correct answer columns, specify the main table, join another table, and define the join condition.
help.hint_2: The the correct answer clause comes after the correct answer to specify how the tables are related.

quiz-inner-join-columns.title: Choosing Columns for INNER JOIN
quiz-inner-join-columns.prompt: Which columns are commonly used to join the `students` and `enrollments` tables?
quiz-inner-join-columns.hint: Look for columns that represent the same entity in both tables.

help.concept: Joins are usually made using primary and foreign keys that relate the same entity across tables.
help.hint_1: The `enrollments` table references students using the correct answer foreign key.
help.hint_2: `students.id` is the primary key in `students`, and `enrollments.student_id` is the foreign key referencing it.

quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.title: Complete the INNER JOIN Statement
quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete this INNER JOIN:

```sql
SELECT students.name, enrollments.term
FROM students
_____ enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
```
quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.hint: Which JOIN type returns only matching rows?

help.concept: the missing term is used to return only rows with matching values in both tables.
help.hint_1: The blank should be filled with the JOIN type that matches rows in both tables.
help.hint_2: LEFT JOIN and RIGHT JOIN include unmatched rows, but the missing term does not.

quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.template: SELECT students.name, enrollments.term FROM students [BLANK] enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;

quiz-inner-join-code.title: Write an INNER JOIN Query
quiz-inner-join-code.prompt: Write a SQL query to list the names of students and the titles of the courses they are enrolled in. Use the `students`, `enrollments`, and `courses` tables.
quiz-inner-join-code.hint: You need to join enrollments to both students and courses.

help.concept: To get student names and course titles, join enrollments to students (by student_id) and to courses (by course_id).
help.hint_1: Start from enrollments, join students on student_id, then join courses on course_id.
help.hint_2: Select students.name and courses.title after joining the three tables.
